name: arch_inv_allows_zero_layers
code:
proof fn phi_1_arch_inv_allows_zero_layers(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
    ensures
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: An arch with zero layers is degenerate and should be excluded by inv, but the constraint `layers.len() <= X86_NUM_LAYERS` does not enforce a positive lower bound

name: entry_base_equals_next_entry_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_entry_base_equals_next_entry_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        idx < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx) == arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: If entry_base equals next_entry_base for a valid arch, entries have zero size contradicting the positive entry_size invariant

name: well_formed_allows_zero_entries
code:
proof fn phi_3_well_formed_allows_zero_entries(dir: Directory)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
    ensures
        dir.entries.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: A directory with zero entries is degenerate; inv should prevent this via num_entries > 0 from arch.inv

name: next_entry_base_not_greater
code:
proof fn phi_4_next_entry_base_not_greater(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        idx < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, idx) <= arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: next_entry_base should always be strictly greater than entry_base under a valid arch; if not, address regions would be empty or inverted

name: subdirectory_base_vaddr_overlap
code:
proof fn phi_5_subdirectory_base_vaddr_overlap(dir: Directory, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        dir.inv(),
        i < dir.entries.len(),
        j < dir.entries.len(),
        i != j,
        dir.entries[i as int] is Directory,
        dir.entries[j as int] is Directory,
    ensures
        dir.entries[i as int]->Directory_0.base_vaddr == dir.entries[j as int]->Directory_0.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: Two distinct subdirectory entries sharing the same base_vaddr would mean overlapping address spaces, violating isolation between page table entries
